title: Code Review: launchers/SecureUSB.bat VeraCrypt installer flow
description: Review of the updated SecureUSB.bat that auto-offers VeraCrypt installation. One file changed (launchers/SecureUSB.bat). Three findings identified: wildcard glob safety, 32-bit install location detection gap, and cosmetic encoding concern.

items.title: Quoted wildcard in `for` loop may not expand on all Windows versions
items.description:
Line 14: `for %%F in ("%~dp0VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt Setup *.exe") do set "INSTALLER=%%F"`

Problem: In CMD, quoting a `for` set item prevents space-splitting but whether wildcards inside the quotes are expanded is implementation-defined and differs across Windows versions. If the glob does NOT expand, `INSTALLER` is set to the literal string `D:\VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt Setup *.exe` (with asterisk), `if not defined INSTALLER` is false (INSTALLER is defined), and `start /wait` tries to launch a file with a literal `*` in its name — which fails silently.

Fix: Use `for /f` with `dir /b` which reliably handles paths with spaces and wildcards:
```batch
for /f "delims=" %%F in ('dir /b "%~dp0VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt Setup *.exe" 2^>nul') do set "INSTALLER=%~dp0VeraCrypt\%%F"
```
This form:
- Uses `dir /b` for bulletproof glob expansion
- Redirects stderr to nul so a missing dir doesn't print an error
- Combines the directory prefix with just the filename from `dir /b`
items.resolution: Fixed: replaced `for %%F in (quoted-glob)` with `for /f \"delims=\" %%F in ('dir /b ... 2^>nul')` for reliable cross-version wildcard expansion.

items.title: `%ProgramFiles%` misses 32-bit VeraCrypt install location
items.description:
Line 9: `set "VC_EXE=%ProgramFiles%\VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt.exe"`

Problem: On a 64-bit Windows system, `%ProgramFiles%` = `C:\Program Files`. If a user previously installed the 32-bit VeraCrypt build (or chose a custom path), it lands in `%ProgramFiles(x86)%` = `C:\Program Files (x86)`. The existing check doesn't find it, so the script proceeds to offer installation — the user may end up running the installer on top of an existing install, potentially causing confusion.

Fix: Add a second existence check before jumping to installer detection:
```batch
set "VC_EXE=%ProgramFiles%\VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt.exe"
if exist "%VC_EXE%" goto already_installed
set "VC_EXE=%ProgramFiles(x86)%\VeraCrypt\VeraCrypt.exe"
if exist "%VC_EXE%" goto already_installed
```
items.resolution: Fixed: added second existence check for %ProgramFiles(x86)%\\VeraCrypt\\VeraCrypt.exe after the primary Program Files check.

items.title: Non-ASCII characters in `::` comments — encoding risk
items.description:
Lines 8, 12: The `──` box-drawing characters and `—` em dash in `::` comments are non-ASCII (Unicode).

Problem: If the .bat file is saved or re-opened in ANSI encoding (Windows legacy default), these characters are corrupted and display as mojibake in a console window. Since they are in `::` comment lines they have zero functional impact, but it looks messy if a user opens a CMD window.

Fix (optional): Replace with ASCII-only comments:
```batch
:: -- Step 1: Check if VeraCrypt is already installed --------------------
:: -- Step 2: Not installed - look for bundled installer -----------------
```
This is cosmetic only — the file currently works correctly if saved UTF-8.
items.approval_note: User chose to keep Unicode — file is UTF-8, cosmetic concern only.
